mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production (INDPRO) indicate expanding manufacturing output and overall economic activity, which drives higher corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises but SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 55% of instances over a rolling 3-year window, or the Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO growth and forward 20d returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0%, it signals labor market deterioration and declining aggregate demand, which reduces corporate profits and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: After UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, SPX delivers positive 60d returns in more than 50% of cases, or the average return in triggered periods is not significantly lower than in non-triggered periods (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal robust consumer spending and residential investment, jointly confirming strong aggregate demand that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST rise month-over-month, the forward 20d SPX return is negative in >50% of cases, or the joint signal does not improve predictive performance over a univariate baseline (e.g., Rank IC < 0.02 on a rolling basis).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation (CPIAUCSL level above 200), the Federal Reserve is inclined to tighten monetary policy. Rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) in this regime compress equity valuations by increasing discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, rising FEDFUNDS is followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or the negative return difference between FEDFUNDS increase and decrease episodes is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent increases in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) erode real purchasing power and raise expectations of future monetary tightening, which dampens equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL rises but SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 55% of months, or the Spearman rank correlation between CPIAUCSL level and forward 60d returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When nominal monthly retail sales (RSAFS) fall below 360,000 million dollars, it indicates abrupt consumer demand weakness that immediately pressures corporate revenues and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: After an RSAFS reading below 360,000, SPX produces positive 5d returns in more than 50% of cases, or the average 5d return in triggered periods is not significantly lower than in non-triggered periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) provide a broad confirmation of economic expansion across both labor and output channels, reinforcing corporate earnings expectations and pushing S&P 500 returns positive over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and INDPRO rise month-over-month, the forward 60d SPX return is negative in >50% of cases, or the joint signal does not produce a positive average return over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of strong economic growth (GDPC1 year-over-year growth exceeding 3%), a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a cooling labor market that prevents overheating and extends the expansion, making it a positive signal for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth exceeds 3%, rising UNRATE is followed by negative 20d SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or the average return under the trigger is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).
